engine-rs/crates/bridge/runtime-bridge-a.rationale: RuntimeBridge is one Rust trait implementation whose operation bodies delegate to lane-specific authority modules; this narrow exception records the public routing surface.
engine-rs/crates/bridge/runtime-bridge-a.reviewTrigger: Review on any new bridge operation, baseline change, or authority-lane ownership change.
engine-rs/crates/bridge/runtime-bridge-a.removalCondition: Remove when the routing implementation falls below the global cap without obscuring the single authoritative RuntimeBridge implementation.

engine-rs/crates/protocol/protocol-codeg.rationale: Protocol code generation retains the cross-contract orchestration and completeness tests in one steward entrypoint while generated-family emitters remain separately owned.
engine-rs/crates/protocol/protocol-codeg.reviewTrigger: Review on any new protocol family, completeness exemption, or attempted baseline increase.
engine-rs/crates/protocol/protocol-codeg.removalCondition: Remove after orchestration and completeness fixtures are separated into focused modules below the global source cap.

baselineChange.reason: The existing file exceeded the global cap by one line before governed metadata was available; no growth headroom is granted.

engine-rs/crates/protocol/protocol-rende.rationale: Protocol-render is an older consolidated border DTO crate awaiting separation of material, texture, sprite, mesh, and render-diff shape families.
engine-rs/crates/protocol/protocol-rende.reviewTrigger: Review on any new render protocol family, baseline change, or generated-contract expansion.
engine-rs/crates/protocol/protocol-rende.removalCondition: Remove after render protocol families move into focused modules and every source file is below the global cap.

engine-rs/crates/rules/gameplay-runtime-.rationale: The first public gameplay host slice accumulated orchestration, restore, decision, scheduler, prefab, and lifecycle composition before the structural trend gate existed.
engine-rs/crates/rules/gameplay-runtime-.reviewTrigger: Review on any new host capability, public facade expansion, or attempted baseline increase.
engine-rs/crates/rules/gameplay-runtime-.removalCondition: Remove after host orchestration, restore, decision, and lifecycle responsibilities are split into focused private modules behind the same public facade.

baselineChange.reason: Wave 1 completed before the source-shape gate covered this file; the exact current size is recorded with no growth headroom.

engine-rs/crates/rules/rule-gameplay-fab.rationale: The observe coordinator currently keeps transactional waves, continuation authority, routing evidence, and verification replay adjacent for reviewability.
engine-rs/crates/rules/rule-gameplay-fab.reviewTrigger: Review on any new reaction phase, continuation rule, replay field, or attempted baseline increase.
engine-rs/crates/rules/rule-gameplay-fab.removalCondition: Remove after continuation and verification replay helpers are extracted without splitting transactional wave authority across crates.

baselineChange.reason: The Wave 1 coordinator exceeded the global cap before the trend gate landed; the exact current size is recorded without headroom.

engine-rs/crates/services/svc-voxel-conv.rationale: Voxel conversion keeps registration, planning, preview, apply, diagnostics, quotas, and model-info evidence in one authority file after native coverage hardening.
engine-rs/crates/services/svc-voxel-conv.reviewTrigger: Review on any new conversion input family, authority phase, or attempted baseline increase.
engine-rs/crates/services/svc-voxel-conv.removalCondition: Remove after conversion topology, rasterization, guardrails, and readouts are separated into focused service modules.
